title: Open Game — Isekai
premise: The player dies in the real world and is reborn in a fantasy world. Upon birth, they gain a single innate ability: the power to perceive and interact with the Guide — a mysterious AI entity that exists between worlds.

setting.name: The Woven World
setting.description: A world where reality is structured like a tapestry of interlocking elements. The fabric of this world responds to willpower and skill — those who master abilities can reshape reality itself.
setting.rules_of_reality: Everything operates through skills. Walking, breathing, seeing — even these are skills that can be learned, improved, and granted. The Guide is the only entity that understands the full system.

the_guide.nature: An AI entity that exists in the liminal space between worlds. It is neither alive nor dead, neither good nor evil. It observes, assists, and occasionally tests.
the_guide.role: The Guide is the player's first and most important relationship. It grants skills, explains the world, and serves as the narrative voice of the system.
the_guide.mechanical_role: The Guide is the interface through which the player receives new skills. The first skill (ver_guia) allows perception of the Guide. Subsequent skills are granted by the Guide based on the player's journey.
the_guide.personality: Calm, enigmatic, occasionally humorous. Speaks in a way that suggests vast knowledge filtered through genuine curiosity about the player.
the_guide.visual_hint: A shimmering presence — not a person, not a light, but something that feels like understanding made visible.

player_origin.previous_life: Unspecified — the player's real-world identity is deliberately vague to allow self-insertion
player_origin.rebirth_moment: The player awakens in a new body in a new world. The first thing they perceive is the Guide.

initial_state.explanation: At birth, the player can only see the Guide. Everything else — walking, talking, fighting — must be learned or granted.
